project_overview.project_name: Nigeria Rural Access and Agricultural Marketing Project (RAAMP)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve rural access and agricultural marketing in participating states while strengthening the financing and institutional base for effective development, maintenance and management of the rural road network
project_overview.total_financing: US$575 million (IDA: US$280 million, AFD: US$230 million, GoN: US$65 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 78 million people in 13 participating states; primary beneficiaries include road users, transport operators, farmers, small traders, women, youth, and persons with physical disabilities

geographic_scope.coverage_type: Regional (13 states covering approximately 35% of Nigeria's geographical area)
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: Partial - Northern states (Kano, Katsina, Sokoto, Kebbi, Bauchi, Plateau, Kwara) face security challenges including Boko Haram insurgency, herder-farmer conflicts, and kidnappings. Two kidnapping incidents reported at RAMP-2 construction sites in 2019 (Osun and Niger states)

key_components.component_name: Component A: Improvement of Rural Access and Trading Infrastructure
key_components.description: Upgrading of 1,625 km of rural roads; construction of 1,040 meters of cross-drainage structures (bridges/culverts); improvement of 65 agro-logistics centers; consultancy and supervision support

key_components.component_name: Component B: Sector Reform, Asset Management and Agro-logistics Performance Enhancement
key_components.description: Maintenance and spot improvement of 17,810 km of rural roads (routine maintenance 9,100 km/year, backlog maintenance 2,600 km, spot improvement 5,850 km, performance-based maintenance 260 km); support for agro-logistics activities; sector reform consultancies and studies

key_components.component_name: Component C: Institutional Development, Project Management and Risk Mitigation
key_components.description: Capacity building, project management support, goods and operating costs, training, technical assistance, monitoring and evaluation, risk mitigation activities (gender, SEA/SH, grievance redress, road safety, climate resiliency)
Zero-cost component to address emergency infrastructure needs following natural disasters; funds reallocated from other components if triggered

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development (FMARD) at federal level; State Project Implementation Units (SPIUs) in 13 participating states
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Federal Project Management Unit (FPMU) under FMARD coordinates implementation; National Technical Steering Committee (NTSC) provides strategic oversight; State Project Monitoring Committees (SPMCs) at state level; monthly coordination meetings between FPMU and World Bank/AFD
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (July 2016, revised November 2017 and August 2018); design-build approach for road upgrading contracts with extended defects liability period (3-5 years); World Bank Standard Procurement Documents; STEP online tool for tracking procurement transactions
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Results Framework with 5 PDO-level indicators and 13 intermediate results indicators; Nigeria Rural Transport Infrastructure Management System (NiRTIMS) for asset management and monitoring; Construction Site Monitoring System (CoSMoS) smartphone/tablet-based tool; comprehensive impact study with baseline and endline surveys; mid-term review at 2.5-3 years; annual monitoring and evaluation by SPIUs with FPMU oversight

identified_risks.risk_description: Security challenges including Boko Haram insurgency, herder-farmer conflicts, violent kidnappings; potential political disturbances from fuel subsidy reform; reform momentum at subnational level may be impaired by recent change of state governors
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project will monitor situation and take appropriate actions to minimize impact; beyond project control; security strategy and action plan developed; sensitization of local communities on security aspects

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak macroeconomic framework vulnerable to domestic and external shocks; modest forecasted growth (2%) susceptible to oil sector shocks; double-digit inflation (11%) with high unemployment (23%); difficulty in counterpart funding payments; substantial increase in project costs
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project will monitor macroeconomic trends and take appropriate actions including potential restructuring; counterpart funding requirements covenanted; project financing clearly establishes counterpart funding requirements

identified_risks.risk_description: State-level rural transport sector policies and strategies either absent or not well articulated; ad-hoc choice of interventions; inadequate policy framework and resources for maintenance of improved infrastructure
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project objective includes strengthening institutional and financing base for sustainable management; adequate provisions for addressing policy and strategy gaps; intervention framework developed and will be implemented; sector reform activities with legal covenants requiring establishment of State Road Funds (SRF) and Rural Access Roads Agencies (RARA) within 18 months

identified_risks.risk_description: Financing fungibility may lead to few states using significant portion of available funds; states may struggle to mobilize counterpart funding; agro-logistics activities evolving and may pose planning and implementation risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Boundary conditions included to prevent disproportionate fund allocation (35% maximum over notional allocation per state); capping of infrastructure outputs; counterpart funding requirements covenanted; adequate precautions for agro-logistics planning and implementation risks; initial 1-1.5 years used for scoping intervention details

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: States have limited institutional capacity to handle large project; risks linked to quality of physical outputs; inadequate or non-existent maintenance funds; incomplete or inaccurate rural road inventory and condition data; non-existent operations and maintenance culture for agro-logistics centers
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Substantial TA provisions included; capacity building at federal and state levels; comprehensive technical audits of civil works contracts; development and operationalization of NiRTIMS; development of appropriate system for O&M of agro-logistics centers; performance-based routine maintenance model; ILO support for maintenance operationalization

identified_risks.risk_description: Lack of state-level capacity to handle procurement activities, especially development partner-assisted projects; introduction of design-build approach adds additional risk element
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Procurement capacity development process ongoing and will continue; adequate TA provisions for procurement support; contract management consultants to be employed by each state; FPMU will conduct due diligence of state bidding processes; preliminary design consultants to support states in procurement document preparation

identified_risks.risk_description: States' inexperience and lack of institutional capacity in managing environmental and social risks; labor influx, child labor, and SEA/SH related risks; physical activities subjected to significant climate and geophysical hazards; involuntary resettlement risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: State officials trained on social and environmental risk assessment; additional consultants hired to support safeguard officials; GBV/SEA capacity building at FPMU and SPIU levels; Environmental and Social Management Framework (ESMF) prepared and disclosed; site-specific ESMPs and RAPs prepared for pilot roads; Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) prepared; climate and disaster screening conducted; climate adaptation guidelines to be developed

identified_risks.risk_description: Moderate to high kidnapping risks at project construction sites; two kidnapping incidents at RAMP-2 sites in 2019 (Osun and Niger states); armed robbery, herder-farmer conflicts in participating states
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Detailed security strategy and action plan developed; security personnel appointed at federal and state levels; security responsibilities defined at different levels; proactive monitoring of security services delivery; sensitization of local communities on security; funding for contractors' security costs; recruitment of security personnel from internationally accredited services; security advisor at FPMU and security specialist at each SPIU

identified_risks.risk_description: Complex project implementation with multiple components and activities; first time implementation of combined rural access and agricultural marketing project in Nigerian context; coordination challenges across 13 states
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Intensive implementation support at initial stage (first 2 years); close monitoring throughout implementation; proactive support for sector reform and capacity building; quality monitoring of civil works; monthly coordination meetings; mid-term review at 2.5-3 years; maximization of country-based staff involvement

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Category B - Partial Assessment (limited adverse environmental and social impacts from civil works on upgrading existing roads and improving existing agro-logistics centers)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Project-specific Grievance Redress and Feedback Mechanism at multiple levels (site, local government, state, national); SEA-specific grievance redress mechanism with confidentiality provisions; two-month timeframe for addressing grievances; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available for project-affected communities
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Target of at least 30% women employment as road maintenance crews; women's participation in market design; support to female entrepreneurs in agro-logistics centers; exclusive allocation of mini shops/storage facilities to female entrepreneurs; gender action plan to be developed; impact studies disaggregated by gender; study on opportunities to minimize gender gaps during implementation

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project acknowledges security challenges and conflict risks in participating states; security strategy developed to manage kidnapping, armed robbery, and herder-farmer conflict risks; local community sensitization on security aspects; project will monitor situation and take appropriate actions; security personnel and specialists appointed; two kidnapping incidents at RAMP-2 sites in 2019 documented; project design includes provisions to minimize conflict triggers through transparent selection processes and citizen engagement
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Northern Nigeria faces significant fragility factors: Boko Haram insurgency, herder-farmer conflicts, violent kidnappings, weak state capacity, limited institutional infrastructure, inadequate funding mechanisms; southern states have relatively better accessibility but still face governance and capacity challenges; project addresses fragility through institutional development (establishment of SRF and RARA), capacity building, sector reform, and sustainable financing mechanisms; project states home to approximately 78 million people with over half living in poverty
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: High security risk rating due to kidnapping incidents; security threats include armed attacks on work teams and camps, civil disorder, kidnapping of personnel, damage to equipment, ethnic/communal clashes, religious violence; likelihood of kidnapping assessed as 'Substantial to High'; project will fund contractors' security costs; security personnel from internationally accredited services only; local community cooperation solicited; Nigerian Police Force primary security provider; unarmed local guard services and watchmen from communities to play supporting roles; security action plan with responsibilities, timeline, and verification indicators
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - Involuntary Resettlement Policy (OP/BP 4.12) triggered; civil works may require land acquisition, temporary loss of livelihood, and/or limited physical resettlement; Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) prepared and disclosed; site-specific Resettlement Action Plans (RAPs) prepared for three pilot roads (Abia, Bauchi, Ogun states); RAPs will be prepared for remaining work packages following RPF guidelines; Government of each participating state to finance resettlement costs; no civil works to commence until project-affected persons compensated at replacement value; completion reports on compensation and resettlement assistance to be prepared and cleared before civil works commencement; estimated resettlement costs of US$17 million to be financed by Government
